name: Sierra del Villar Loop
name_original: Río Ruecas por la Sierra del Villar — circular desde Rena
description: Jog this moderate 7.2-mile (11.7 km) circular route from Rena, exploring the Río Ruecas and Sierra del Villar area in 1 hour and 25 minutes.
region_intro: Jogging around Rena offers a diverse landscape, characterized by river plains and undulating terrain. The region features routes along greenways and through areas with moderate elevation changes, suitable for various running preferences. Trails often follow river courses and explore local hills, providing varied scenery for runners. Best jogging routes around Rena The most popular jogging route is Vía Verde Vegas del Guadiana to Las Villuercas — circular by the Río Guadiana, a 9.0 miles (14.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 35 minutes to complete. This route follows a greenway, offering a relatively flat and scenic path along the Río Guadiana. Another top favourite among local runners is Sierra del Villar Loop, a moderate 7.2 miles (11.7 km) path. This loop navigates through the Sierra del Villar, featuring varied terrain and some elevation gain. Local runners also love the Running loop from Villanueva de la Serena, a 6.1 miles (9.8 km) trail leading through local landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 2 minutes. Jogging around Rena is defined by river paths, greenways, and local hills, offering a mix of flat and moderately challenging terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy runs to more demanding routes with elevation changes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 140 runners have used komoot to explore Rena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of running routes can I find around Rena?
A: Jogging around Rena offers a diverse landscape, characterized by river plains and undulating terrain. You'll find routes along greenways, river courses like the Río Guadiana, and through local hills, providing a mix of flat and moderately challenging paths suitable for various running preferences.

Q: How many jogging trails are available in the Rena area?
A: There are over 25 dedicated jogging routes around Rena listed on komoot. These include a variety of options, from easy strolls to more challenging runs with elevation changes.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly running routes in Rena?
A: Yes, Rena offers several easy jogging routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1282810779/via-verde-vegas-del-guadiana-to-las-villuercas-circular-by-the-rio-guadiana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde Vegas del Guadiana to Las Villuercas — circular by the Río Guadiana&lt;/a&gt; is a greenway route that is relatively flat and scenic, making it a great option for beginners or a relaxed run. Overall, there are 3 easy routes available.

Q: Can I find circular running routes around Rena?
A: Absolutely. Many of the routes around Rena are designed as loops. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1284542110/sierra-del-villar-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra del Villar Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which offers varied terrain and some elevation gain, providing a complete running experience without retracing your steps.

Q: What interesting sights or landmarks can I see while jogging near Rena?
A: While running around Rena, you can encounter several points of interest. Routes often pass by the &lt;strong&gt;Río Guadiana&lt;/strong&gt; and the &lt;strong&gt;Ruecas River&lt;/strong&gt;. You might also spot the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1813279" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadiana River Viaduct&lt;/a&gt; or explore areas near &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6080107" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villanueva de la Serena&lt;/a&gt;. For a unique natural feature, look for &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6087621" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Palmeral&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any running routes that follow rivers or have water features?
A: Yes, many running routes in the Rena area incorporate water features. The region is characterized by river plains, and trails often follow river courses. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1282810779/via-verde-vegas-del-guadiana-to-las-villuercas-circular-by-the-rio-guadiana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde Vegas del Guadiana to Las Villuercas&lt;/a&gt;, for example, runs along the Río Guadiana. You can also find routes near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5966896" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruecas River in Palazuelo&lt;/a&gt; and even the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5980750" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entrerríos Riverside Beach&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Rena?
A: Given the region's characteristics, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for jogging. Summers can be quite warm, especially in river plains, so early mornings or late evenings would be preferable. Winters are typically mild, making year-round running possible, though some days might be cooler.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running routes suitable for jogging with kids?
A: For families, routes with gentler terrain and scenic views are ideal. The greenway sections, such as parts of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1282810779/via-verde-vegas-del-guadiana-to-las-villuercas-circular-by-the-rio-guadiana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde Vegas del Guadiana&lt;/a&gt;, are generally flat and safe, making them suitable for jogging with children. Always check the specific route details for distance and elevation to ensure it matches your family's ability.

Q: What do other runners say about jogging in Rena?
A: The running routes around Rena are popular among the komoot community, with over 140 runners having explored the area. Users often appreciate the varied terrain, from flat river paths to routes with moderate elevation, and the scenic landscapes that define the region.

Q: Are there any longer running routes for experienced runners?
A: Yes, for those looking for a longer challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31848026" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Haba loop from Villanueva de la Serena&lt;/a&gt; offer a substantial distance of 8.5 miles (13.7 km) with moderate difficulty, providing a good workout for experienced runners.

Q: Where can I find parking near the running trails in Rena?
A: While specific parking information for every trail isn't always available, routes often start near towns or accessible points. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20130552" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Villanueva de la Serena&lt;/a&gt; would likely have parking options within or near the town itself, making it convenient to access the trail.
tips: Rena is located at the foot of what is called the Sierra del Villar, a small mountain system that runs along the foot of Río Ruecas and the town, right in the middle.

faqs.question: Where does the Río Ruecas por la Sierra del Villar circular route start?
faqs.answer: This circular jogging route begins and ends in Rena (Badajoz). You can typically find parking within the town itself, making it convenient to access the trailhead.

faqs.question: Is this jogging trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The trail is rated as moderate. While it's a good length at just under 12 km, the elevation gain is manageable at around 140 meters. Beginners with a reasonable level of fitness should find it enjoyable, but be prepared for some gentle ascents.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to jog this route is generally during the spring (March to May) or autumn (September to November). Temperatures are milder, and the natural scenery is often at its most vibrant. Summers can be very hot in this region, making early mornings or late evenings preferable if visiting then.

faqs.question: Is the Río Ruecas por la Sierra del Villar trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through populated areas like Rena (Badajoz) or if there's livestock nearby. Always carry water for your pet, especially on warmer days.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to use this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to access and enjoy the Río Ruecas por la Sierra del Villar circular route. It's freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this jogging route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, typical of a rural route in the Sierra del Villar. You can expect a mix of dirt paths, gravel tracks, and possibly some paved sections as you pass through or near Rena (Badajoz). Good jogging shoes with decent grip are recommended.

faqs.question: Are there any viewpoints or natural features along the way?
faqs.answer: While specific named viewpoints aren't highlighted, the route follows the Río Ruecas and traverses the Sierra del Villar, offering pleasant natural scenery. You'll experience views of the river, local flora, and the gentle hills of the Sierra.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. You will find sections that overlap with the Camino Mozárabe por Trujillo (covering about 44% of the route), as well as parts of the Camino de Gamonal and Camino de Pasaderas.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Río Ruecas por la Sierra del Villar circular route?
faqs.answer: On average, joggers can expect to complete this 11.6 km circular route in about 1 hour and 25 minutes. This duration can vary depending on your pace and any stops you make along the way.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restrooms near the trailhead in Rena?
faqs.answer: As the trail starts and ends in Rena (Badajoz), you should be able to find local amenities such as cafes, bars, or small shops within the town for refreshments or restroom facilities before or after your run.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a jogging trip on this trail?
faqs.answer: For this moderate jogging route, we recommend bringing appropriate running shoes, comfortable athletic clothing, a water bottle (especially in warmer months), and sun protection like a hat and sunscreen. A small snack might also be useful for sustained energy.
